title: Microsoft 365 Administrator
summary: Microsoft 365 tenant administration, Microsoft Entra identity, Defender XDR security operations, and Microsoft Purview compliance.

objectives.name: Deploy and manage a Microsoft 365 tenant
Implement and manage Microsoft Entra identity and access
Manage security and threats by using Microsoft Defender XDR
Manage compliance by using Microsoft Purview

questions.prompt: An MSP is adding a customer's existing DNS domain to Microsoft 365. The domain currently handles production email elsewhere, so mail flow must not change during ownership verification. Which DNS record should normally be added first?
questions.explanation: A TXT record can verify domain ownership without changing mail routing. Changing the MX record redirects email and should be done only when the mail-flow migration is ready.
questions.sourceName: Add a custom domain to Microsoft 365

choices.text: The Microsoft-provided TXT verification record
The Microsoft 365 MX record
The Exchange Online Autodiscover CNAME record
The Microsoft 365 SPF TXT record

questions.prompt: Operations staff need notifications only for Exchange Online incidents affecting one customer tenant. Where should a tenant administrator configure this?
questions.explanation: Service Health in the Microsoft 365 admin center provides tenant-specific advisories and incidents, and admins can customize email notification preferences by service and issue type.

choices.text: Microsoft 365 admin center Service Health notification preferences
Microsoft Entra Connect Health alert settings
Microsoft Defender XDR alert notification rules
Microsoft Purview DLP alert policies

questions.prompt: Users at one branch report poor Microsoft 365 performance. The MSP wants tenant-specific measurements and recommendations for that office's network path to Microsoft 365. Which capability should be reviewed?
questions.explanation: Microsoft 365 network connectivity provides location-level assessments, measurements, and recommendations for connectivity to Microsoft 365 services.
questions.sourceName: Network connectivity in the Microsoft 365 admin center

choices.text: Network connectivity insights in the Microsoft 365 admin center
Microsoft Entra Connect Health synchronization latency
Defender for Cloud Apps Cloud Discovery traffic logs
Microsoft 365 usage analytics in Power BI

questions.prompt: A customer enables Microsoft 365 Backup for Exchange Online. Which configuration determines which mailboxes are protected?
questions.explanation: Microsoft 365 Backup uses protection policies for each supported workload. An Exchange protection policy defines the mailboxes included in backup protection.

choices.text: An Exchange protection policy in Microsoft 365 Backup
An Exchange retention policy in Microsoft Purview
A Defender for Office 365 preset security policy
A Microsoft 365 Apps servicing profile

questions.prompt: A Microsoft 365 license is assigned to a group, but one member does not receive the service plans. Where should the administrator look for the user's inherited assignment error?
questions.explanation: Group-based licensing records assignment state and errors for individual users. Common causes include insufficient available licenses, conflicting service plans, or an invalid usage location.

choices.text: The user's group-based license assignment state and error details
The group's Microsoft 365 expiration policy status
The user's Defender XDR identity timeline
The group's sensitivity label publication status

questions.prompt: A 25 GB shared mailbox is used by a support team. It does not use an archive and is not placed on hold. Which licensing statement is correct?
questions.explanation: A shared mailbox generally does not require its own license when it stays within the unlicensed shared-mailbox limits and does not use features that require licensing. Users accessing it still need appropriate licenses.

choices.text: The shared mailbox generally needs no separate license, but its users need licensed Exchange Online access
The shared mailbox always needs the same license as every delegated user
The shared mailbox needs a license only because more than one user accesses it
Neither the shared mailbox nor its delegated users require Exchange Online licenses

questions.prompt: A regional service desk must manage user properties only for users in its own business unit. Which Microsoft Entra design provides scoped delegation?
questions.explanation: An administrative unit contains the regional objects, and a supported role assignment scoped to that unit limits the administrator's authority to those objects.
questions.sourceName: Administrative units in Microsoft Entra ID

choices.text: Place the users in an administrative unit and scope a supported role assignment to it
Place the users in a Microsoft 365 group and make the service desk group owners
Place the users in a dynamic group and assign User Administrator tenant-wide
Place the users in an access package catalog and assign Catalog Owner

questions.prompt: MSP administrators need occasional Exchange Administrator access in a customer tenant. Approval, MFA, justification, and a two-hour limit are required. Which approach removes standing privileged access?
questions.explanation: PIM eligible assignments allow just-in-time activation. Role settings can require approval, MFA, justification, and enforce a maximum activation duration.
questions.sourceName: Configure Microsoft Entra role settings in PIM

choices.text: An eligible Exchange Administrator assignment in PIM with the required activation settings
An active Exchange Administrator assignment reviewed every quarter
A role-assignable group with permanent active membership
An administrative unit scoped Global Reader assignment

questions.prompt: Before the first synchronization from AD DS to Microsoft Entra ID, an administrator needs to find duplicate and incorrectly formatted directory attributes that could cause synchronization errors. Which tool should be run?
questions.explanation: IdFix scans on-premises Active Directory for common object and attribute problems that can prevent successful synchronization to Microsoft Entra ID.
questions.sourceName: IdFix directory synchronization error remediation tool

choices.text: IdFix
Microsoft Entra Connect Health
Microsoft Support and Recovery Assistant
Microsoft 365 network connectivity test

questions.prompt: A customer has multiple disconnected AD DS forests and wants lightweight synchronization agents with cloud-managed configuration and active-agent availability. Which solution best matches?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Entra Cloud Sync uses lightweight provisioning agents, supports disconnected forests, and can use multiple active agents for availability.
questions.sourceName: What is Microsoft Entra Cloud Sync?

choices.text: Microsoft Entra Cloud Sync
Microsoft Entra Connect Sync with one active server per tenant
Microsoft Entra cross-tenant synchronization
Microsoft Entra Application Proxy

questions.prompt: The operations team needs alerts and health information for Microsoft Entra Connect Sync servers and synchronization services. Which service should they use?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Entra Connect Health provides monitoring and alerts for supported hybrid identity components, including Connect Sync.

choices.text: Microsoft Entra Connect Health
Microsoft 365 Service Health
Microsoft Defender XDR system health
Microsoft Purview Data Explorer

questions.prompt: Synchronized users reset their passwords in Microsoft Entra self-service password reset. The new passwords must also be written to on-premises AD DS. What must be enabled?
questions.explanation: Password writeback sends supported cloud password changes and resets to on-premises AD DS through the configured hybrid synchronization service.
questions.sourceName: How self-service password reset writeback works

choices.text: Password writeback
Password hash synchronization
Pass-through authentication
Seamless single sign-on

questions.prompt: The tenant-specific banned-password list must be enforced for password changes on on-premises domain controllers. Which two on-premises components are required?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Entra Password Protection for AD DS uses proxy services to retrieve policy and DC agents to validate password changes on domain controllers.
questions.sourceName: Microsoft Entra Password Protection for AD DS

choices.text: Microsoft Entra Password Protection proxy service
Microsoft Entra Password Protection DC agents
Microsoft Entra Application Proxy connectors
Microsoft Entra Connect Health agents for AD FS

questions.prompt: A policy requiring MFA for administrators must be evaluated against real sign-ins before enforcement. Which Conditional Access policy state records results without applying the grant control?
questions.explanation: Report-only mode evaluates the policy during sign-ins and records what the result would have been, without enforcing the policy controls.

choices.text: Report-only
On with an empty target resource list
Off with sign-in logs enabled
On with session controls only

questions.prompt: A medium-or-higher sign-in risk should be remediated during authentication when the legitimate user can complete MFA. Which Conditional Access condition and control should be combined?
questions.explanation: A sign-in risk condition can target risky authentication attempts, and requiring MFA allows the legitimate user to remediate the sign-in risk. User risk represents likely identity compromise and is handled differently.
questions.sourceName: Risk policies in Microsoft Entra ID Protection

choices.text: Sign-in risk of medium or high with the Require multifactor authentication control
User risk of medium or high with the Require compliant device control
Insider risk of medium or high with the Require password change control
Device risk of medium or high with the Require authentication strength control

questions.prompt: Which account type should normally be excluded from broad Conditional Access policies and separately monitored for every sign-in?
questions.explanation: Dedicated emergency access accounts provide a recovery path when normal authentication or policy dependencies fail. Their usage should be exceptional and generate alerts.

choices.text: Cloud-only emergency access accounts
All eligible PIM administrator accounts
All synchronized service accounts
All external collaboration accounts

questions.prompt: Alerts involving the same user, endpoint, and malicious email are generated by several Microsoft Defender products. Where should an analyst investigate the correlated attack as one case?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Defender XDR correlates related alerts and evidence across products into incidents, providing a unified investigation and response view.

choices.text: The Microsoft Defender XDR incident queue
The Microsoft 365 Service Health dashboard
The Microsoft Purview Activity explorer
The Microsoft Entra provisioning logs

questions.prompt: An analyst must proactively search Defender XDR telemetry across endpoints, email, identities, and cloud applications with a reusable query. Which capability should be used?
questions.explanation: Advanced hunting provides a KQL-based query environment over Microsoft Defender XDR data and supports saving queries and creating custom detections when appropriate.

choices.text: Advanced hunting with Kusto Query Language
Microsoft Purview Content search with Keyword Query Language
Microsoft Entra sign-in diagnostics with Log Analytics disabled
Microsoft 365 usage reports with Power BI filters

questions.prompt: A security lead wants prioritized recommended actions and points that measure the organization's security posture across Microsoft 365. Which feature should be reviewed?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Secure Score provides improvement actions and a score reflecting implemented security configurations and activities. It does not replace risk assessment or guarantee that a tenant is secure.

choices.text: Microsoft Secure Score
Identity Secure Score only
Exposure score for one device
Microsoft 365 adoption score

questions.prompt: A URL in an email was harmless at delivery but became malicious before the recipient selected it. Which Defender for Office 365 feature is designed to evaluate the link when selected?
questions.explanation: Safe Links rewrites or checks supported links and performs time-of-click URL evaluation, helping protect against links whose reputation changes after delivery.
questions.sourceName: Safe Links in Microsoft Defender for Office 365

choices.text: Safe Links time-of-click protection
Safe Attachments dynamic delivery
Anti-phishing mailbox intelligence
Zero-hour auto purge only

questions.prompt: Email bodies should reach recipients promptly while attachments are being detonated. The attachment should become available only after scanning completes successfully. Which Safe Attachments action fits?
questions.explanation: Dynamic Delivery can deliver the message body while the attachment is scanned, then reattach a safe file after analysis completes.
questions.sourceName: Safe Attachments in Microsoft Defender for Office 365

choices.text: Dynamic Delivery
Block
Replace
Monitor

questions.prompt: Attackers send messages from look-alike addresses that impersonate the customer's chief executive. Which Defender for Office 365 policy capability should be configured?
questions.explanation: Anti-phishing policies can protect specified users and domains from impersonation and use mailbox intelligence and spoof intelligence as additional signals.

choices.text: User impersonation protection in an anti-phishing policy
URL detonation in a Safe Links policy
Attachment detonation in a Safe Attachments policy
Outbound spam limits in an anti-spam policy

questions.prompt: A customer wants a controlled credential-harvest simulation followed by targeted security training, without sending a real malicious campaign. Which feature should be used?
questions.explanation: Attack Simulation Training provides Microsoft-managed simulation techniques, payloads, landing pages, user targeting, and training experiences within Defender for Office 365.

choices.text: Attack Simulation Training
Advanced hunting custom detections
Microsoft Purview Insider Risk Management analytics
Microsoft Entra authentication registration campaign

questions.prompt: A mailbox exceeded outbound sending limits after compromise and now appears as a restricted entity. What should happen before the user is unblocked?
questions.explanation: A restricted sender should be investigated and the compromise remediated first, including securing the account and removing malicious persistence. Unblocking without remediation permits continued abuse.
questions.sourceName: Remove blocked users from Restricted entities

choices.text: Investigate and remediate the account compromise, then remove the user from Restricted entities
Remove the user from Restricted entities, then wait for another outbound alert before investigating
Delete the outbound spam policy, then restore it after the user signs in
Add the user to the Tenant Allow/Block List to bypass outbound filtering

questions.prompt: Managed Windows endpoints should be onboarded to Microsoft Defender for Endpoint at scale through Intune. Which Intune policy area is intended to deploy the onboarding configuration?
questions.explanation: After establishing the Defender for Endpoint connection, Intune endpoint detection and response policy can deploy onboarding settings to targeted supported devices.
questions.sourceName: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint with Intune

choices.text: Endpoint security endpoint detection and response policy
Device compliance policy with machine risk disabled
App protection policy for Microsoft Defender
Enrollment Status Page blocking app list

questions.prompt: Defender Vulnerability Management finds an outdated application on many Intune-managed devices. The security analyst wants to send a traceable remediation request to endpoint administrators. What should the analyst create?
questions.explanation: A security recommendation can be submitted as a remediation request to Intune, allowing security and endpoint teams to track the activity and remediation status.

choices.text: A remediation request from the security recommendation to Microsoft Intune
A Secure Score improvement action assigned directly to each device
A Cloud Discovery sanction action for the application
A Defender for Office 365 submission for the installer

questions.prompt: An MSP wants to discover unsanctioned cloud applications used from a customer network by analyzing firewall or proxy traffic. Which Defender for Cloud Apps capability should be configured?
questions.explanation: Cloud Discovery analyzes traffic logs from supported network devices or integrated Defender for Endpoint signals to identify cloud app usage and associated risk.

choices.text: Cloud Discovery with a supported log collector or data source
Conditional Access App Control without a connected application
Microsoft Purview Audit with endpoint events disabled
Microsoft Entra Application Proxy connector traffic reports

questions.prompt: A customer-specific account number follows a stable pattern and must be detected in DLP policies. The built-in sensitive information types do not match it. What should be created?
questions.explanation: A custom sensitive information type can combine a regular expression or keyword pattern with supporting evidence and confidence requirements for use in Purview policies.
questions.sourceName: Create a custom sensitive information type

choices.text: A custom sensitive information type using a regular expression and appropriate supporting evidence
A sensitivity label using a regular expression as its encryption key
A retention label using adaptive scope syntax
A trainable classifier containing only the account-number format

questions.prompt: Files marked Confidential must remain encrypted when emailed outside the organization. The requirement is about protection, not how long the files are kept. Which Purview control should be used?
questions.explanation: Sensitivity labels classify and protect content and can apply encryption and usage rights. Retention labels govern preservation and deletion rather than access protection.

choices.text: A sensitivity label configured with encryption
A retention label configured to retain forever
A DLP policy configured only to audit
A records-management file plan descriptor

questions.prompt: Users may work with sensitive documents on managed Windows devices, but copying those files to personal USB storage must be blocked. Which Purview capability directly controls this endpoint activity?
questions.explanation: Endpoint DLP extends Purview DLP controls to onboarded devices and can audit or restrict activities such as copying sensitive content to removable media.
questions.sourceName: Learn about Endpoint data loss prevention

choices.text: Endpoint DLP with a rule restricting copy to removable USB devices
Defender for Endpoint device control without a content condition
A sensitivity label policy that publishes a USB label
A retention policy for Windows device locations

questions.prompt: A compliance administrator needs both an inventory of locations containing labeled content and a timeline of user labeling activities. Which two Purview tools should be used?
questions.explanation: Content explorer shows an item-oriented view of classified or labeled content and its locations. Activity explorer shows events and user actions related to labels and data protection.

choices.text: Content explorer
Activity explorer
Cloud Discovery
Advanced hunting

questions.prompt: An MSP wants Microsoft 365 Apps updates managed from the cloud, with a validation group receiving each update before the broad population and with deployment health visible centrally. Which capability should be configured?
questions.explanation: Cloud Update in the Microsoft 365 Apps admin center manages update deployment and health for supported channels. Rollout waves let administrators validate an update with an earlier group before broader deployment.

choices.text: Microsoft 365 Apps Cloud Update with staged rollout waves
Microsoft 365 Service Health notification preferences
A Microsoft Purview retention policy with adaptive scopes
Windows Autopilot pre-provisioning

questions.prompt: A customer wants trend-based insight into how employees use collaboration and mobility capabilities, with recommendations intended to improve adoption rather than raw per-user audit events. Which Microsoft 365 report fits?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Adoption Score provides aggregated people-experience and technology-experience insights, trends, and recommendations. Audit records and Defender incidents address security or investigation rather than adoption outcomes.

choices.text: Microsoft Purview Audit
Microsoft Secure Score
Microsoft Adoption Score
Microsoft Entra provisioning insights

questions.prompt: An MSP must create users from a controlled CSV in many customer tenants and record success or failure for each row. The legacy AzureAD and MSOnline modules must not be introduced. Which automation interface should be used?
questions.explanation: The Microsoft Graph PowerShell SDK provides supported cmdlets for Microsoft Entra and Microsoft 365 directory operations and can be scripted to process input and capture per-object results. The AzureAD and MSOnline modules are legacy.

choices.text: Exchange Online PowerShell New-Mailbox for every cloud user
The Microsoft Graph PowerShell SDK with least-privilege scopes
The MSOnline module with tenant-wide Global Administrator
A Microsoft Purview content search import job

questions.prompt: A project team needs a shared mailbox and calendar plus membership-backed access to a SharePoint team site. Which group type should the administrator create?
questions.explanation: A Microsoft 365 group provides a shared mailbox, calendar, SharePoint resources, and a common membership model for collaboration. A distribution list is mail-only, and a security group does not create the collaboration services.

choices.text: A mail-enabled security group
A distribution list with delivery management
A dynamic administrative unit
A Microsoft 365 group

questions.prompt: Users need an external supplier's address to appear in the Exchange Online global address list. The supplier must not receive sign-in access or a mailbox in the tenant. What should be created?
questions.explanation: A mail contact represents an external email address in the organization's address book without creating a sign-in identity or hosted mailbox. A guest user is used when the person needs resource access.
questions.sourceName: Manage mail contacts in Exchange Online

choices.text: A B2B guest user with Exchange Administrator
A mail contact
A shared mailbox forwarded to the supplier
A room mailbox hidden from address lists

questions.prompt: A compliance analyst must investigate DLP alerts but must not edit DLP policies or receive broad Microsoft Entra directory permissions. Where should the least-privilege permissions be assigned?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Purview role groups combine role permissions for compliance tasks. Assigning the analyst to an appropriate built-in or custom Purview role group avoids granting a broad Microsoft Entra administrative role.

choices.text: A Microsoft Entra Security Administrator assignment
A Microsoft Purview role group containing only the required investigation roles
A SharePoint site collection administrator assignment
An Exchange Online Organization Management assignment

questions.prompt: An indirect provider needs its support group to administer a customer's users for six months. The relationship must use selected Microsoft Entra roles, be time bound, and avoid tenant-wide standing Global Administrator access. What should the partner request?
questions.explanation: Granular delegated admin privileges provide time-bound partner access based on selected Microsoft Entra roles and mapped partner security groups. DAP is broader and does not provide the same granular least-privilege model.

choices.text: A granular delegated admin privileges relationship with the required roles and duration
A delegated administrative privileges relationship assigning Admin Agents indefinitely
A B2B guest account permanently assigned Global Administrator
An Azure Lighthouse delegation at management-group scope

questions.prompt: A second Microsoft Entra Connect Sync server should maintain current import and synchronization data but must not export while the primary server is healthy. Which configuration supports a controlled failover?
questions.explanation: Staging mode allows Connect Sync to import and synchronize without exporting changes or performing password synchronization. An administrator deliberately changes staging state during a planned failover.
questions.sourceName: Microsoft Entra Connect Sync staging server

choices.text: Enable staging mode on the secondary server
Enable pass-through authentication on both servers and export from both
Place the secondary server in an Application Proxy connector group
Disable the scheduler on both servers

questions.prompt: A synchronization filtering mistake unexpectedly removes thousands of objects from scope. Which Microsoft Entra Connect Sync safeguard can stop the export before the cloud deletions are applied?
questions.explanation: Prevent accidental deletes stops an export when pending deletions exceed the configured threshold. It gives administrators time to investigate and correct filtering before explicitly allowing a legitimate large deletion.

choices.text: The duplicate attribute resiliency feature
The accidental-deletion threshold
Microsoft Entra Connect Health email notifications only
The soft-match hardening setting

questions.prompt: The tenant still uses legacy per-user MFA and SSPR method settings. Administrators want one converged policy for method enablement and a staged migration without immediately changing enforcement for all users. Which policy should they use?
questions.explanation: The Microsoft Entra authentication methods policy is the converged control for authentication method enablement. Migration states support evaluating and moving from legacy MFA and SSPR method policies in stages.

choices.text: Authentication methods policy with the appropriate migration state
A Conditional Access named location migration
A registration campaign with legacy policies left permanently authoritative
An app protection policy for Microsoft Authenticator

questions.prompt: Users should register security information once and use the registered methods for both multifactor authentication and self-service password reset. Which experience should be enabled?
questions.explanation: Combined security information registration gives users one registration experience for MFA and SSPR methods. The applicable authentication-method and SSPR policies still determine which methods each user can use.

choices.text: Separate per-user MFA proof-up and SSPR registration portals
Password writeback through Cloud Sync
Combined registration for MFA and SSPR
A Temporary Access Pass for every recurring sign-in

questions.prompt: A Conditional Access policy should distinguish sign-ins from the customer's fixed public office egress addresses from other locations. The addresses must be reusable across several policies. What should be created?
questions.explanation: A named location can contain trusted or untrusted IP ranges or countries and can be reused as a Conditional Access location condition. It does not by itself grant access; each policy decides how to use it.

choices.text: A device filter containing the office router addresses
An authentication context named for the office
A cross-tenant access trust for the office
An IP-based named location

questions.prompt: Privileged portal access must accept passkeys and certificate-based authentication but reject SMS even when SMS would otherwise satisfy MFA. Which Conditional Access grant control should be configured?
questions.explanation: A Conditional Access authentication strength restricts the accepted authentication method combinations. The built-in phishing-resistant MFA strength excludes methods such as SMS while accepting supported passkeys and certificate-based authentication.

choices.text: Require multifactor authentication
Require phishing-resistant MFA authentication strength
Require a compliant device only
Require an approved client app

questions.prompt: An analyst has verified account takeover through evidence outside Identity Protection. The user currently has a low risk state. Which administrator action records the conclusion and raises the user risk?
questions.explanation: Confirm user compromised records the analyst's determination and sets user risk to high. Dismissing user risk or confirming a sign-in safe would incorrectly treat the activity as legitimate.

choices.text: Confirm sign-in safe
Dismiss user risk
Confirm user compromised
Mark the device compliant

questions.prompt: Executives require Microsoft's most aggressive recommended Defender for Office 365 baseline, while the broader organization remains on the less restrictive recommended baseline. Which policy design fits?
questions.explanation: Preset security policies provide Standard and Strict recommended protection profiles. Strict protection can target the executive group while Standard protection covers the broader population, with exclusions planned to avoid overlap issues.

choices.text: Target the executives with Strict preset security policy and the broader population with Standard
Target executives with Standard and disable anti-phishing for everyone else
Use only the Built-in protection preset because it is the strictest profile
Create a Safe Links policy and assume it replaces all other threat policies

questions.prompt: After confirming a phishing campaign, an analyst must find matching delivered messages across recipient mailboxes and initiate removal from the same investigation workflow. Which Defender for Office 365 capability should be used?
questions.explanation: Threat Explorer provides detailed email threat investigation, filtering, message selection, and remediation actions such as soft delete or hard delete, subject to permissions and action approval requirements.

choices.text: Microsoft 365 Service Health
Microsoft Purview Content explorer
Threat Explorer in Defender for Office 365
Cloud Discovery in Defender for Cloud Apps

questions.prompt: An automated investigation has completed and recommends removing a malicious file, but the tenant is configured to require approval before remediation. Where should an analyst approve or reject the action?
questions.explanation: Automated investigation and response places remediation actions that require review in Action center as pending actions. Authorized analysts can inspect evidence and approve or reject them there.

choices.text: The Action center Pending tab
The Microsoft 365 admin center Message center
The Purview Data Explorer
The Entra admin consent request queue

questions.prompt: A tested advanced-hunting query identifies suspicious behavior. Analysts want it evaluated automatically on a schedule and want matching results to create alerts with entity context. What should they create?
questions.explanation: A custom detection rule runs a supported advanced-hunting query at a configured frequency and creates alerts or incidents from matches. Saving the query alone does not schedule detection.

choices.text: A saved advanced-hunting query only
A Microsoft Purview alert policy
A Defender XDR custom detection rule based on the query
A Secure Score improvement action

questions.prompt: A Windows endpoint is actively communicating with an attacker. Analysts must cut off normal network communication while preserving supported Defender for Endpoint communication for continued response. Which device action should they use?
questions.explanation: The Isolate device action restricts device network connectivity while maintaining Defender for Endpoint service communication needed for monitoring and response. Intune Retire removes management data but is not an active containment action.
questions.sourceName: Take response actions on a device

choices.text: Retire the device from Intune
Isolate the device in Defender for Endpoint
Disable the device object only in Microsoft Entra ID
Revoke the primary user's sessions only

questions.prompt: A connected SaaS app should generate an alert when a user downloads an unusually large number of files in a short period, and the policy should optionally suspend the user after a match. Which Defender for Cloud Apps policy type fits?
questions.explanation: An activity policy evaluates activities from connected apps using filters and repeated-activity thresholds and can trigger governance actions such as suspending a user where supported. A file policy evaluates files rather than activity volume.

choices.text: A file policy based only on sensitivity labels
A Cloud Discovery snapshot report
An activity policy with repeated-activity thresholds and a governance action
A Conditional Access sign-in frequency policy

questions.prompt: An incident contains a suspicious IP address. The analyst wants Microsoft Defender Threat Intelligence context about infrastructure, related indicators, and observed associations before deciding whether it belongs to a known campaign. Where should the indicator be investigated?
questions.explanation: Defender Threat Intelligence Intel Explorer and search experiences provide intelligence context for indicators such as IP addresses, domains, and URLs, including related infrastructure and observed associations.
questions.sourceName: Search and investigate with Defender Threat Intelligence

choices.text: Microsoft 365 network connectivity insights
Defender Threat Intelligence indicator search or Intel Explorer
Microsoft Purview Content explorer
Microsoft Entra Connect Health

questions.prompt: During an endpoint investigation, an authorized analyst must open a remote command session to collect a file and run an approved remediation script on an onboarded device. Which Defender for Endpoint capability provides this?
questions.explanation: Live response provides a secured remote shell for authorized responders to investigate and remediate onboarded devices, including collecting files and running approved scripts according to role permissions and settings.
questions.sourceName: Investigate entities on devices using live response

choices.text: Intune device query
Defender for Endpoint live response
Microsoft Entra sign-in diagnostics
Defender for Office 365 Threat Explorer

questions.prompt: A SharePoint document is subject to one policy that retains it for seven years and another policy that would delete it after three years. No record label changes the behavior. What happens at year three?
questions.explanation: When retention settings conflict, retention takes precedence over deletion. The document remains preserved until the longest applicable retention period is satisfied; deletion can occur only afterward according to the applicable configuration.

choices.text: The document is permanently deleted because deletion actions always win
The policies cancel each other and the document follows the recycle-bin defaults
The document remains preserved because the seven-year retention requirement takes precedence
The three-year policy automatically converts the document into a regulatory record

questions.prompt: A DLP rule should warn users before they email sensitive data externally. Authorized users may continue for a valid business reason, but they must enter a justification that compliance staff can review. Which rule behavior should be configured?
questions.explanation: A DLP policy tip can notify the user and allow an override with business justification. The override event and justification are available for auditing and investigation; audit-only mode would not require the interaction.
questions.sourceName: Send notifications and show policy tips for DLP

choices.text: Audit the event silently without user notification
Block with a policy tip and allow override only with a business justification
Apply a retention label and allow users to delete it
Encrypt the message with a sensitivity label and disable auditing

questions.prompt: A high-severity DLP alert spans Exchange Online and endpoint events. The analyst needs the matched policy, affected content, user activity, and a place to assign and update investigation status. Which Purview workspace should be used first?
questions.explanation: The DLP Alerts dashboard provides alert and event details, affected entities and content context, assignment, and investigation workflow for DLP policy matches across supported locations.

choices.text: Microsoft 365 Service Health
Microsoft Entra risky users
Defender for Cloud Apps Cloud Discovery
The Microsoft Purview DLP Alerts dashboard

questions.prompt: An MSP is adding a customer's existing email domain to Microsoft 365. The registrar does not support Domain Connect, and production mail must not be redirected before Microsoft 365 proves ownership. Which sequence is correct?
questions.explanation: The administrator first adds the domain in the Microsoft 365 admin center, publishes the provided ownership-verification record, and verifies ownership. Service DNS records such as MX and CNAME records are added only after verification, and the completed configuration is then validated. Publishing the production MX record before ownership verification can cause an avoidable mail-flow cutover.
questions.sourceName: Add a custom domain to Microsoft 365

choices.text: Add the domain in Microsoft 365; publish the verification record; verify ownership; publish the required service DNS records; validate domain setup
Publish the Microsoft 365 MX record; add the domain; verify ownership; publish the verification record; validate domain setup
Add the domain; publish all service DNS records; validate mail flow; publish the verification record; verify ownership
Publish the verification record; publish the MX record; add the domain; validate setup; verify ownership
Add the domain; verify ownership before publishing any verification record; publish service records; validate setup

questions.prompt: An MSP is onboarding a new CSP customer with time-bound, least-privileged delegated administration. Technicians must use their partner identities through a dedicated security group. Which sequence correctly establishes GDAP access?
questions.explanation: The partner prepares a security group and membership, requests a GDAP relationship containing the required roles and duration, and sends the customer the approval link. Only after customer approval can the partner map the approved roles to that security group and verify active access. Assigning groups before approval or placing technicians in broad Admin Agents access bypasses the intended least-privilege design.
questions.sourceName: Obtain granular admin permissions for a customer

choices.text: Create the partner security group and add technicians; request the GDAP roles and duration; obtain customer approval; assign approved roles to the group; verify active access
Request GDAP; assign roles to the group; create the group; obtain customer approval; add technicians; verify access
Create the group; obtain customer approval without a role request; add technicians; request roles; verify access
Create customer-local technician accounts; request GDAP; obtain approval; create the partner group; assign roles
Add technicians to Admin Agents; assign customer Global Administrator; request GDAP; obtain approval; create the scoped group
Create the group and add technicians; request GDAP; assign approved roles before customer approval; obtain approval; verify access

questions.prompt: A customer sends mail from its Microsoft 365 custom domain and from an approved third-party service. The MSP wants recipients to validate authorized sending sources, message signatures, and alignment with the visible From domain. Choose three email-authentication technologies to configure together.
questions.explanation: SPF identifies authorized sources for the envelope sender, DKIM signs messages, and DMARC evaluates SPF or DKIM alignment with the visible From domain and defines failure handling. ARC can preserve authentication results through trusted intermediaries but does not replace the domain owner's SPF, DKIM, and DMARC deployment; Safe Links and anti-malware policies address different threats.

choices.text: SPF
DKIM
DMARC
Authenticated Received Chain as a replacement for DMARC
Safe Links time-of-click protection
A malware filter policy

questions.prompt: A customer wants Microsoft-recommended Defender for Office 365 settings. Executives can tolerate tighter filtering, while all remaining users need the normal recommended baseline. Choose two assignments that meet the requirement with minimal custom-policy maintenance.
questions.explanation: The Strict preset security policy is intended for selected high-value or high-risk users who can accept more aggressive protection, while Standard provides the recommended baseline for other users. Custom anti-phishing and Safe Attachments policies would add maintenance and can introduce precedence complexity without satisfying the request for preset recommendations.

choices.text: Assign the Strict preset security policy to the executive group
Assign the Standard preset security policy to the remaining users
Assign the Strict preset policy to every user and use a transport rule to relax it
Disable preset security policies and reproduce every recommendation with custom policies
Assign Standard to executives and Built-in protection only to everyone else

questions.prompt: An MSP must introduce a tenant-wide Conditional Access policy requiring MFA while minimizing lockout and disruption. Which rollout order follows Microsoft guidance?
questions.explanation: Emergency access exclusions and authentication readiness are prerequisites. Microsoft recommends piloting new policies in report-only mode, reviewing sign-in impact, and only then enabling enforcement for the wider population. Enabling tenant-wide first or adding emergency exclusions afterward creates a preventable lockout risk.

choices.text: Verify emergency access exclusions and user authentication readiness; target a pilot in report-only mode; review sign-in results; expand and enable enforcement
Enable enforcement tenant-wide; review failures; add emergency exclusions; reduce scope to a pilot
Target a pilot in report-only mode; enable tenant-wide enforcement; then register authentication methods and exclude emergency accounts
Exclude emergency accounts; enable tenant-wide enforcement; review sign-in results; then create a pilot group

questions.prompt: Defender XDR correlates credential theft with malware execution on one onboarded Windows laptop. The SOC must immediately stop that device from communicating with other corporate systems while retaining its connection to Defender for investigation. Which response action should be taken first on the device?
questions.explanation: Isolate device restricts network communication while preserving connectivity to Microsoft Defender for continued investigation and response. Starting live response or collecting a package can help investigate but does not provide the required containment; suppressing an alert changes visibility rather than device communication.
questions.sourceName: Device entity response actions in Defender XDR

choices.text: Isolate device
Initiate a live response session without isolation
Collect an investigation package
Suppress the device's alerts
Offboard the device from Defender for Endpoint
Exclude the detected file indicator globally

questions.prompt: A new Purview DLP policy will block external sharing of regulated identifiers across SharePoint and OneDrive. The customer requires evidence of likely impact and tuning before user activity is blocked. Which deployment sequence is correct?
questions.explanation: The policy should be scoped and configured first, then run in simulation mode. Administrators review matched items and simulation alerts, tune conditions or exceptions, and only then enable enforcement. Turning on blocking before simulation violates the stated change-control requirement, while reviewing production alerts cannot substitute for pre-enforcement evidence.

choices.text: Configure the scoped policy; run it in simulation mode; review matches and alerts; tune conditions and exceptions; enable enforcement
Enable enforcement; review blocked events; switch to simulation; tune exceptions; re-enable enforcement
Configure the policy; tune exceptions without results; enable enforcement; run simulation; review matches
Run simulation before defining locations; enable enforcement; configure rules; review alerts; tune conditions
Configure the policy; enable enforcement for all users; review matches; tune; then limit the location scope

questions.prompt: A customer's approved GDAP relationship contains User Administrator and Service Support Administrator. Only the MSP identity team should receive User Administrator, and technicians must not get broader standing roles. Choose two partner-side actions.
questions.explanation: GDAP maps customer-approved Microsoft Entra roles to security groups in the partner tenant, so the MSP should use a dedicated group and add only the identity team members to it. Admin Agents or customer-local accounts would broaden or duplicate access, and a partner security group cannot receive roles outside the customer-approved relationship.
questions.sourceName: Grant GDAP roles to security groups

choices.text: Map User Administrator to a dedicated partner security group in the active GDAP relationship
Add only the MSP identity team members to that dedicated security group
Add all technicians to the partner Admin Agents group
Create permanent customer-local Global Administrator accounts for the identity team

questions.prompt: Adding a Microsoft Entra group to an administrative unit brings the group object into the unit's management scope, but it doesn't automatically bring the group's users into that scope.
questions.explanation: The administrative unit scopes management of the group object and its membership. Individual users must also be direct administrative-unit members before scoped administrators can manage those users.
questions.sourceName: Administrative units in Microsoft Entra ID

choices.text: True
False

questions.prompt: Microsoft Teams chat messages can be retained with a Microsoft Purview retention policy, but retention labels aren't supported for Teams messages.
questions.explanation: Teams chats and channel messages are supported retention-policy locations. Teams messages don't support retention labels, which are intended for supported item-level content such as documents and email.

choices.text: True
False

questions.prompt: A tenant can keep Security defaults enabled while replacing those protections with custom Conditional Access policies.
questions.explanation: Organizations that replace Security defaults with Conditional Access policies must disable Security defaults, then implement policies that maintain the required protections.
questions.sourceName: Security defaults for Microsoft Entra ID

choices.text: True
False

questions.prompt: Defender for Endpoint security settings management fully enrolls previously unmanaged devices into Intune, so they can receive every Intune configuration profile.
questions.explanation: Security settings management lets supported devices receive selected Intune endpoint-security policies without full Intune enrollment. Unsupported Intune profiles and capabilities still require Intune enrollment.

choices.text: True
False
